text:
User: btw, docs/wiki should be committed in each repo after you regenerate it

Assistant: Got it — I'll commit `docs/wiki/` in each repo after its backfill. A few specifics on how I'll do it safely:

- **Scoped add only** — `git add docs/wiki/` (not `-A`), so I never touch any *other* uncommitted work-in-progress in your repos. Commit message like `docs(wiki): regenerate project wiki via proactive-context archeologist` + the Claude co-author line.
- **On the repo's current branch, commit-only (not push)** — you said "committed," so I'll commit locally. Say the word if you also want them pushed.
